title: Add a within-lap ranking model for pit urgency
summary: Pit calls are partly relative: teams react to competitors in the same race and lap, not just absolute tyre age.
rationale: Pit calls are partly relative: teams react to competitors in the same race and lap, not just absolute tyre age. Ranking objectives are designed for learning within-group orderings, and CatBoost supports query/groupwise losses that can learn which driver is most likely to pit among cars sharing a lap context.
implementation_hint: Train a second model with `GroupId=(Year,Race,LapNumber)` or a slightly wider race-lap bucket, target still binary, and optimize a ranking loss such as `QuerySoftMax`/`YetiRankPairwise` or LambdaRank in a library that supports grouped ranking. Blend its OOF scores with the standard binary classifier.
expected_effect: Should improve relative ordering among simultaneous candidates, which can help ROC AUC even if absolute probabilities are imperfect.
risk: Some groups may contain very few positives or none, making ranking noisy; it is a complement, not a replacement, for the binary model.
